english_track_name: Renewable energy systems; Sustainable nuclear energy; DENSYS 2.0 - Decentralised smart ENergy SYStems - Erasmus Mundus Joint Master; HySET - Hydrogen Systems and Enabling Technologies - Erasmus Mundus Joint Master [uncertain]
double_degree_or_erasmus_mundus: The programme includes DENSYS 2.0 and HySET Erasmus Mundus Joint Master tracks in the 2026/2027 curriculum; the 2025/2026 programme academic regulations explicitly state that HySET has separate admission requirements and procedures. The programme page also advertises Twin Track double-master opportunities for students in Renewable Energy Systems or Design and Management of Power Plants. A current 2026/2027 PoliTo call/fee page specifically for DENSYS 2.0 was not found among the checked official sources. [uncertain]
admission_test_type: Academic evaluation of qualifications, curricular background, and language requirements on Apply@polito; no programme-specific written entrance exam was found for the standard programme.
applicant_route: Applicants with an Italian qualification from Politecnico di Torino
Applicants with an Italian qualification from other Italian universities
Applicants with a non-Italian qualification who are EU citizens or non-EU citizens residing in Italy
Applicants with a non-Italian qualification who are non-EU citizens residing abroad and need a study visa
admission_call_round: Foreign-qualification route: 1st Call and 2nd Call for a.y. 2026/2027.
Italian-qualification route: first-semester and second-semester application windows for a.y. 2026/2027.
HySET specialist track: separate admission requirements and procedures.

DENSYS 2.0 appears in the provisional 2026/2027 curriculum as an Erasmus Mundus track, but a current 2026/2027 PoliTo admission-call page for that track was not found among the checked official sources. [uncertain]
application_platform: Apply@polito for the admission application; UNIVERSITALY for non-EU applicants residing abroad who need visa pre-enrolment.

academic_requirements.general: Admission requires a three-year Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification recognized as suitable for admission, fulfilment of curricular requirements, and academic performance considered suitable for admission.
academic_requirements.programme_background: For the English Energy and Nuclear Engineering entry in the 2026/2027 course-specific requirements, the recommended background is Industrial engineering, and the syllabus is required.
academic_requirements.italian_qualification_route: Applicants from other Italian universities must have a weighted average grade of at least 24/30. Applicants from Politecnico di Torino are assessed under the programme rules based on time-to-degree and, where applicable, weighted average grade.
academic_requirements.foreign_qualification_route: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification are evaluated by an Academic Committee / designated professors for consistency between prior studies and the chosen programme.

country_specific_eligibility: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification must hold a qualification awarded by an accredited/recognized foreign university after at least 15 years of total education. Applicants still enrolled in a 5- or 6-year single-cycle programme may apply only if they still meet 15 total years of education, including at least 3 university years, and have earned at least 180 ECTS or equivalent.
minimum_background_or_credits: According to the programme academic regulations currently linked from the programme page (Academic Year 2025/2026), applicants to the standard tracks must have at least 40 credits in the first SSD group (CHIM/07, FIS/01, FIS/03, ING-INF/05, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05) and at least 60 credits in the second SSD group (including FIS/04, ICAR/01, ICAR/03, ICAR/08, ING-IND/08, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 18, 19, 22, 24, 25, 31, 32, 33). Applicants lacking fewer than 10 credits may still be admitted by the Academic Advisor; if the gap is more than 10 credits, final approval is escalated. This specific credit framework comes from the 2025/2026 regulations, because a 2026/2027 programme-specific regulations PDF was not found among the checked sources. [uncertain]

language_requirements.english: English B2 or above is required. In the 2026/2027 course-specific requirements PDF, the English-taught Energy and Nuclear Engineering programme states a minimum requirement of IELTS 5.5.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: The official PoliTo B2 certificate table includes IELTS Academic/General Training 5.5, Pearson PTE Academic 59, TOEFL iBT (the table shows 72 for tests obtained before 21/01/2026), TOEIC (Listening 400, Reading 385, Speaking 160, Writing 150), and Cambridge B2/C1/C2 certificates accepted under the published minimum conditions.
language_requirements.italian: Italian is not required for the English programme entry; Italian B2 appears on the separate partially Italian programme entry, not on the English one.

english_certificate_codes: TOEFL institution code: C285. The engineering-area admissions page also instructs applicants to enter the TOEFL Appointment Number on Apply@polito.
language_submission_timing: Applicants with an Italian qualification may submit the admission application without the English certificate, but they must upload it by the enrolment deadline; otherwise they may enrol only in first-semester single courses and must obtain the certificate by 24 March 2027. For foreign-qualification applicants, the English certificate is part of the compulsory enrolment documents and is described as already uploaded during the application process.

application_deadline_eu.italian_qualification_route: First semester: application deadline 15 October 2026; enrolment deadline 12 November 2026. Second semester: application deadline 24 February 2027; enrolment deadline 24 March 2027.
application_deadline_eu.foreign_qualification_eu_or_non_eu_resid: 1st Call closing: 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time. 2nd Call closing: 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time. First-semester online enrolment closes 5 October 2026. Second-semester online enrolment (only for EU and non-EU applicants residing in Italy) closes 24 March 2027.

application_deadline_non_eu.foreign_qualification_non_eu_requiring_v: 1st Call closing: 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time. 2nd Call closing: 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time. First-semester online enrolment closes 5 October 2026. The second-semester enrolment window is stated as only for EU and non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

universitaly_preenrolment_deadline: The same 2026/2027 foreign-qualification page contains conflicting first-call study-visa pre-enrolment deadlines: the deadlines table says 15 May 2026, while the visa section says 30 May 2026. Both parts agree on 15 July 2026 for the second call. [uncertain]
application_fee: No application fee was identified on the 2026/2027 foreign-qualification page checked for this programme. Applicants from other Italian universities in the engineering-area Master's admission procedure must pay a non-refundable EUR 50.00 application fee on Apply@polito. [uncertain]
required_documents: Course syllabus for previous studies (required in the 2026/2027 course-specific requirements entry for Energy and Nuclear Engineering).
Passport, or national ID for EU students.
Residence permit for non-EU citizens living in Italy, or study visa type D for non-EU citizens not living in Italy.
Bachelor's degree (EQF level 6) or, for eligible applicants still in a single-cycle programme, transcript covering at least the first 3 academic years as specified by PoliTo.
Declaration of Value, or Statement of Comparability / Verification from CIMEA or another ENIC-NARIC centre as applicable, or Diploma Supplement / ARDI correspondence certificate where accepted.
Confirmation of eligibility letter for UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment.
document_translation_authentication: For foreign qualifications, degree and transcript documents require official translation into Italian unless the originals are in English, French, or Spanish, or the applicant holds a Diploma Supplement where accepted. PoliTo states that official translations into Italian must either be certified by the Italian Embassy in the issuing country or sworn by a translator in an Italian court. PoliTo also requires Declaration of Value or CIMEA/ENIC-NARIC comparability evidence as specified on the enrolment page, and notes it may request additional verification documents.

entry_exam_or_test.standard_programme: No programme-specific written or oral entrance exam was found for the standard Energy and Nuclear Engineering admission route. Admission is based on evaluation of curricular requirements, academic performance, and language requirements.
entry_exam_or_test.special_tracks: HySET has separate admission requirements and procedures according to the programme academic regulations. DENSYS 2.0 is listed in the provisional curriculum as an Erasmus Mundus Joint Master track.

test_score_or_threshold: There is no standard entrance-test score for the programme. Key thresholds found in official sources are: applicants from other Italian universities need a weighted average grade of at least 24/30; applicants from Politecnico di Torino are assessed under programme rules tied to time-to-degree and, when applicable, weighted average grade (21/30 or 24/30); the programme academic regulations currently linked from the programme page also state the 40-credit and 60-credit SSD minimums for standard tracks.

source_year: Primary admission sources are 2026/2027; the programme-specific academic regulations used for SSD/credit thresholds and the HySET separate-procedure note are 2025/2026. [uncertain]
uncertainty_notes: The 2026/2027 curriculum page states that the listed curricula are provisional and that official curricula are expected in June 2026; the identified English tracks are therefore current but still provisional.

The programme page currently links a 2025/2026 academic regulations PDF. Exact 2026/2027 programme-specific SSD/credit thresholds were not found in an updated regulations PDF among the checked official sources.
The 2026/2027 foreign-qualification page contains conflicting first-call UNIVERSITALY study-visa pre-enrolment deadlines: 15 May 2026 in the deadlines table and 30 May 2026 in the visa section.

HySET clearly has separate admission requirements and procedures in the official programme regulations. DENSYS 2.0 appears on the 2026/2027 curriculum page as an Erasmus Mundus Joint Master track, but a current 2026/2027 PoliTo call or fee page specifically for DENSYS 2.0 was not found among the checked official sources.

The current PoliTo tuition-fee page available in English is the page for students enrolled from academic year 2025/2026 onward; a dedicated 2026/2027 tuition-fee page was not identified among the checked official sources.

The application-fee information found in official sources clearly covers applicants from other Italian universities (EUR 50.00), but no programme-specific foreign-qualification application fee was identified on the 2026/2027 page checked for this programme.
